questions.question:
A remote-controlled stunt drone with a mass of $4.50\text{ kg}$ flies in a flat, horizontal circle at a constant speed of $26.0\text{ m/s}$. It takes the drone $12.0\text{ s}$ to complete one full revolution.

What is the centripetal force keeping it in orbit?

A small $4.50\text{ g}$ coin rests on a vintage vinyl record player at a distance of $12.5\text{ cm}$ from the central spindle. The record spins at a uniform rate of $33.3\text{ rev/min}$.

What is the magnitude of the centripetal force holding the coin in place?

An insect with a mass of $0.850\text{ g}$ hitches a ride on the outer edge of a flat rotating laboratory platform. The insect is positioned exactly $18.0\text{ cm}$ away from the axis of rotation. The platform spins steadily at $78.0\text{ rev/min}$.

Find the centripetal force acting on the insect.

The planet Mars orbits the Sun at an average distance of $2.28 \times 10^{11}\text{ m}$. It takes Mars exactly $687$ days to complete one full revolution around the Sun.

Calculate its orbital tangential speed in meters per second.

An exoplanet completes a circular orbit around its host star once every $42.0$ days. The orbital radius of this planet is $3.80 \times 10^{10}\text{ m}$.

Find its linear tangential speed in meters per second.

A prototype motorcycle speeds along a circular test track with a radius of $85\text{ m}$. The rider completes one full lap in exactly $14.0\text{ s}$.

What is the magnitude of the motorcycle's centripetal acceleration?

A bobsled rounds a smooth, flat circular turn of radius $42.0\text{ m}$ while sustaining a continuous centripetal acceleration of $18.5\text{ m/s}^2$.

What is the time period required for the bobsled to complete a full circular revolution?

A centrifuge module in a research lab spins an experimental component at a radius of $0.650\text{ m}$ from the central pivot point. The unit is configured to pull a steady centripetal acceleration of $115\text{ m/s}^2$.

Determine the time period of one complete rotation.

An engineering student spins a small payload attached to a rigid cable overhead in a horizontal circular orbit. The cable has a radius of $2.4\text{ m}$ and completes $75.0$ full rotations every minute.

What is the constant tangential speed of the payload?

A calibration weight attached to a horizontal tether line of length $0.85\text{ m}$ is whirled continuously inside a test enclosure. The weight registers a steady rotation rate of $160$ complete revolutions per minute.

Calculate its linear velocity magnitude.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered multi-step circular motion calculations!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review using $a_c = 4\pi^2 r/T^2$ and converting between period, frequency, and rpm.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on unit conversions (grams to kg, cm to m, rpm to rad/s) and $v = 2\pi r/T$.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: $v = 2\pi r/T$, $a_c = v^2/r$, $F_c = mv^2/r$, and angular velocity $\omega = 2\pi/T$.
